source_pr.title: expression: Quit builtin function SLEEP when KILLed.
source_pr.body:
Use cancellation pattern in SLEEP function by making use of
transaction's context. But, it does not quit when Ctrl-C
pressed. Add TODO for future investigation.

Fix #4378 partially

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to async timing/cancellation behavior: the builtin `SLEEP()` used `time.Sleep` and did not react to query/session cancellation (KILL), so tests that expected SLEEP to abort could hang or time out depending on timing. This PR stabilizes it by making `SLEEP()` wait via `select` on `time.After(dur)` or `ctx.Done()` (and returning 1 when killed), plus adding a unit test with a cancelable mock context to assert the early-exit behavior.
fix_pattern: Make time-based waits responsive to cancellation: replace plain `time.Sleep` with `select { case <-time.After(...); case <-ctx.Done(): ... }` and add a test that cancels the context.

evidence: evidence: // return 1 when SLEEP() is KILLed
